findings: Unqualified audit opinion issued. Three management letter points raised relating to subsidiary governance documentation and investment valuation methodology.
recommendations: Strengthen subsidiary board reporting requirements; update investment valuation policy to align with IFRS 13; implement quarterly portfolio valuation reviews.
managementResponse: All three management letter points accepted. Remediation plan submitted to audit committee with Q2 2025 completion target.

findings: Audit in progress. Preliminary findings indicate emphasis of matter paragraph likely on mine rehabilitation fund adequacy.
managementResponse: Management engaging with auditors on rehabilitation fund valuation methodology.

findings: Unqualified opinion. Emphasis of matter on going concern related to below-cost-recovery tariffs and GRZ subsidy dependency. Four management letter points on procurement controls and IT general controls.
recommendations: Strengthen procurement committee oversight; implement IT access controls review; progress tariff application to ERB.
managementResponse: Procurement controls enhanced. IT access review completed. Tariff application submitted to ERB in February 2025.

findings: Qualified opinion due to inability to verify completeness of fixed asset register for track infrastructure. Going concern emphasis of matter. Six management letter points on revenue recognition, fleet maintenance records, and safety compliance documentation.
recommendations: Commission independent fixed asset verification; implement fleet management system; strengthen safety compliance documentation.
managementResponse: Fixed asset verification commissioned. Fleet management system procurement in progress. Safety documentation remediation plan submitted.

findings: Three medium-risk findings: (1) Related-party transaction approval process not consistently followed in two subsidiaries; (2) Investment monitoring reports not submitted on time by three portfolio companies; (3) Board minutes not circulated within required 5-day period in one subsidiary.
recommendations: Enforce related-party transaction policy; implement automated monitoring report reminder system; strengthen board secretariat processes in subsidiaries.
managementResponse: All findings accepted. Remediation actions assigned to relevant subsidiary CEOs with 60-day completion deadline.
